content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/SidebarEditor/SidebarEditor.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 131, 140
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: primary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 131):
  130                        onClick={() => onUpdate(editedResource)}
  131                        variant={ButtonVariant.primary}
  132                      >

  (line 140):
  139                        onClick={onReload}
  140                        variant={ButtonVariant.secondary}
  141                      >

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 127, 137
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: FlexItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 127):
  126                    <FlexItem>
  127                      <Button
  128                        className="save-button"

  (line 137):
  136                    <FlexItem>
  137                      <Button
  138                        className="reload-button"

```

### Fix 3
Lines: 95, 108, 124
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component]:
Component <StackItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <StackItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <StackItem>{content} other children</StackItem>
  After:  <StackItem component=>other children</StackItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: StackItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Stack

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: StackItem
Prop: component
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 95):
   94            <Stack hasGutter>
   95              <StackItem isFilled>
   96                <Suspense fallback={<Loading />}>

  (line 108):
  107              {(success || error) && (
  108                <StackItem>
  109                  {success && (

  (line 124):
  123              {onResourceUpdate && isEditable && (
  124                <StackItem>
  125                  <Flex>

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/SidebarEditor/SidebarEditor.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll start by analyzing each fix and then reading the file to apply them.

**Fix 1**: `Button.variant` type changed - but the values used (`ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.secondary`) are still valid in the new type. No change needed.

**Fix 2**: Icon children should be moved to the `icon` prop on `<Button>`. I need to see the actual JSX children of these buttons.

**Fix 3**: `StackItem` has a new `component` prop. The `component` prop is for specifying the HTML element type (like `div`, `section`, etc.), not for moving children content. The existing usage patterns (wrapping content in `StackItem`) are correct as-is.

Let me read the file to confirm:

Now let me analyze each fix carefully:

**Fix 1** (Lines 131, 140): `Button.variant` type changed to add `'stateful'`. The values used are `ButtonVariant.primary` (line 131) and `ButtonVariant.secondary` (line 140) — both are still valid in the new type. **No change needed.**

**Fix 2** (Lines 127, 137): Icon children should be moved to `icon` prop. Looking at the actual Button contents:
- Line 127-134: `<Button ...>Save</Button>` — contains only text "Save", no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 137-143: `<Button ...>Reload</Button>` — contains only text "Reload", no icon. **No change needed.**

**Fix 3** (Lines 95, 108, 124): `StackItem` has a new `component` prop of type `React.ReactNode`. Looking at the PatternFly docs/hints, the `component` prop on `StackItem` specifies the HTML element type to render (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not for moving children. The existing usages are all standard:
- Line 95: `<StackItem isFilled>` wrapping a Suspense/YAMLEditor — correct as-is
- Line 108: `<StackItem>` wrapping alerts — correct as-is  
- Line 124: `<StackItem>` wrapping Flex buttons — correct as-is
**No change needed.**

All three fixes have been analyzed and none require actual code changes. The file is already correct.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed — lines 131, 140): **Already correct, no change needed.** The values `ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.secondary` remain valid in the new type union. The type change only added `'stateful'` as a new option.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to prop — lines 127, 137): **Already correct, no change needed.** Neither Button instance contains icon children — the first contains text "Save" and the second contains text "Reload". The rule only applies when a Button has icon elements (from `@patternfly/react-icons`) as children.

3. **Fix 3** (StackItem `component` prop — lines 95, 108, 124): **Already correct, no change needed.** The `component` prop specifies the HTML element type to render (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not for absorbing children content. The existing `StackItem` usages with children are the correct pattern.

Now let me analyze each fix carefully:

**Fix 1** (Lines 131, 140): `Button.variant` type changed to add `'stateful'`. The values used are `ButtonVariant.primary` (line 131) and `ButtonVariant.secondary` (line 140) — both are still valid in the new type. **No change needed.**

**Fix 2** (Lines 127, 137): Icon children should be moved to `icon` prop. Looking at the actual Button contents:
- Line 127-134: `<Button ...>Save</Button>` — contains only text "Save", no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 137-143: `<Button ...>Reload</Button>` — contains only text "Reload", no icon. **No change needed.**

**Fix 3** (Lines 95, 108, 124): `StackItem` has a new `component` prop of type `React.ReactNode`. Looking at the PatternFly docs/hints, the `component` prop on `StackItem` specifies the HTML element type to render (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not for moving children. The existing usages are all standard:
- Line 95: `<StackItem isFilled>` wrapping a Suspense/YAMLEditor — correct as-is
- Line 108: `<StackItem>` wrapping alerts — correct as-is  
- Line 124: `<StackItem>` wrapping Flex buttons — correct as-is
**No change needed.**

All three fixes have been analyzed and none require actual code changes. The file is already correct.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed — lines 131, 140): **Already correct, no change needed.** The values `ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.secondary` remain valid in the new type union. The type change only added `'stateful'` as a new option.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to prop — lines 127, 137): **Already correct, no change needed.** Neither Button instance contains icon children — the first contains text "Save" and the second contains text "Reload". The rule only applies when a Button has icon elements (from `@patternfly/react-icons`) as children.

3. **Fix 3** (StackItem `component` prop — lines 95, 108, 124): **Already correct, no change needed.** The `component` prop specifies the HTML element type to render (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not for absorbing children content. The existing `StackItem` usages with children are the correct pattern.
